This deal gives you the most expansive power solution (normally $10,597 MSRP and $8,499 at Wellbots) for the brand at $800 less than the price it is currently discounted to across multiple marketplaces, with the included monitor coming free of charge and valuing $349. In terms of the power station, this is the third-lowest price we have tracked, behind the exclusive $6,999 and $6,899 pricing we secured earlier in 2026. items.title: Hyrule Warriors Age of Imprisonment Switch 2 from $40, Metroid Prime 4 35% off, more
items.summary:
Alongside a host of notable deals on Switch 1 and 2 games below, we have a couple more courtesy of Best Buy to add to the mix this morning. Alongside the reveal of the new Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom Mineru’s Construct amiibo (more details here and pre-orders are now live), we are featuring a deal on Hyrule Warriors: Age of Imprisonment on Nintendo Switch 2. The new amiibo is also compatible with this regularly $70 game, and you can now land it down at $49.99 (or $39.99 for My Best Buy Plus members). This deal also joins Best Buy’s offer on Metroid Prime 4: Beyond down at $39.99 on Switch from the regular $60 price tag. Prime 4 was a bit of a disappointment for some, but at this price I think it’s worth a go – scoring the Switch version is a good way to save some cash on it too.

items.title: Lectric Memorial Day Sale offers up to $694 in FREE gear + up to $500 price cuts on e-bikes starting from $799
items.summary:
Lectric has just launched its Memorial Day e-bike Sale, one of the brand’s biggest sale events of the year, not to mention of summer, and you’re able to score up to $720 total savings: up to $694 in FREE bundled gear alongside two models getting up to $500 price drops. Many of the brand’s popular e-bikes are getting increased bundle packages during this event, like my personal pick for all-around commuting versatility, the XP4 750 Folding Utility e-bike that comes with $473 in FREE gear at $1,299 shipped, while the standard XP4 e-bike is getting a FREE $227 bundle at $999 shipped. Without the savings on the bundles, these packages would normally run you $1,772 and $1,226, respectively. The standard XP4 bundles contain the usual LevelUp rack, suspension seat post, and accordion bike lock, while the XP4 750 e-bike gets those alongside an Elite headlight, a phone mount, a pair of mirrors, and a water-resistant pannier bag. Head below to learn more (or check out our hands-on review here) and browse the full lineup of Memorial Day deals.

Already down from $2,799 and $3,499 price tags, you’ll currently find both options as low as $1,619 (at Amazon for the standalone station) and $2,399 (direct from Jackery for bundle) elsewhere, with these prices only beaten by the former exclusive deals we secured for them back during New Years.
